Enum Class Key

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<Key>
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.event.input.Key
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<Key>, Constable
public enum Key extends Enum<Key>
Represents a key on the keyboard. This enum class should generally not be used to determine the actual typed character.

Note that there are different names and/or typical behaviour for several modifier keys and the Menu key, as listed below:

Modifier Keys' Typical Behaviour and Name on Various Platforms
Key named as it is in this class Windows Linux macOS Notes
Shift Shift Shift Shift Same function across all OSes.
Control Control Control Control Less common for app shortcuts on macOS.
Alt Alt Alt Option Generally the same function.
Meta Windows Super (typically) Command Key used for OS-level functions, the main shortcut key on macOS.
Menu Context menu/Application key Menu N/A This key is typically not present on macOS, where the context menu functionality is accessed differently. Pressing this key still can be detected on macOS, though.

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class Enum

      public static final Key MENU
      The menu (AKA Context menu or Application (on Windows)) key. Typically not present on Mac.

  • Method Details

    • values

      public static Key[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
      Returns:
      an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared

    • valueOf

      public static Key valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null

    • getUSMapping

      public char getUSMapping()
      Returns the US mapping for this key, if any, else throws an exception. Most printable keys have a US mapping, but not all of them; most function keys do not have a US mapping, but not all of them.
      Returns:
      the US mapping for this key, if any
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if there is no US mapping for the given key

    • hasUSMapping

      public boolean hasUSMapping()
      Returns whether this key has a US mapping character. In other words, true if getUSMapping() would return a value, or false if it would throw an exception.
      Returns:
      true if this key has a US mapping, false otherwise
